## format() function

Create a formatted string that subscribes itself to all specified parent stores through a one-way binding. If one of the parent stores dispatches an update, the store with the formatted string reformats itself automatically and will notify its subscribers of the string.

Tip: Stores work best if you only use them for binding a model to your user interface, and nowhere else.

This utility store uses OpenRCT2's `context.formatString()` under the hood, and thus supports the same tokens, for example: \* Numbers: `{COMMA32}`<!-- -->, `{INT32}`<!-- -->, `COMMA2DP32` \* Strings: `{STRING}` \* Localised strings: `{STRINGID}` \* Localised currency: `{CURRENCY}`<!-- -->, `{CURRENCY2DP}` \* Localised in-game dates: `{MONTH}`<!-- -->, `{MONTHYEAR}`<!-- -->, `{REALTIME}`<!-- -->, `{DURATION}` \* Localised ride metrics: `{LENGTH}`<!-- -->, `{VELOCITY}` \* Inline sprites: `{SPRITE}`<!-- -->, `{INLINE_SPRITE}` \* Colours: `{RED}`<!-- -->, `{YELLOW}`<!-- -->, `{WINDOW_COLOUR_1}` \* Font sizes: `{MEDIUMFONT}`<!-- -->, `{SMALLFONT}`<!-- -->, `{TINYFONT}`

**Signature:**

```typescript
declare function format<T>(format: string, store: Store<T>): Store<string>;
```
